fields.description:
## Why
Verdict-flip on identical code is a RECALL problem: in flipping same-material review pairs the decisive
would-block findings had validity ~0.99 and 14/17 flips were because the other run simply MISSED the valid
finding. Improving finder recall closes the gap; tightening thresholds cannot.

## What
Feed this ticket's prior-review findings (from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar corpus, matched by `norm_id`) into
Pass-1 as clearly-delimited "PRIOR-REVIEW CONCERNS (re-verify against the CURRENT plan; some may be
resolved)". The finder is prompted to independently re-establish each against the current plan, not to
restate it. Dedup against the fresh findings by `norm_id` happens AFTER Pass-1. The Pass-2 verifier is the
UNCHANGED false-positive backstop: a concern the current plan resolved fails validity and is dropped, so a
stale primed finding cannot block.

## Structural-independence invariant (explicitly preserved)
The codebase deliberately enforces finder independence (each finding/chunk judged without cross-
contamination; guarded by the plan-review parity/isolation tests). This design PRESERVES it: prior findings
enter only as an external, labelled context block (not merged into the current finding set), the per-finding
verification remains independent, and NO finding blocks on memory alone (Pass-2 must independently confirm).
If the injection cannot be done without violating the parity/isolation test, the fallback is a separate
post-Pass-1 recall step over prior norm_ids — but the primary approach must keep the existing tests green.

## Scope
- Pass-1 context assembly + prior-sidecar load only; Pass-2/Pass-3 unchanged. Cost is context tokens, not
  extra LLM calls. Scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pass-1 receives the ticket's prior-review findings as labelled re-verify candidates, deduped by
      `norm_id`; they are NOT injected into the current finding set pre-verification.
- [ ] The existing finder-independence / parity-isolation test(s) still pass (invariant preserved).
- [ ] No additional LLM calls beyond the existing finder pass (context injection only).
- [ ] Test: a finding present in a prior review but dropped on a re-run is re-surfaced; a resolved prior
      finding is dropped by Pass-2, not re-blocked. Recall lift measured on same-`material_fingerprint` pairs.
